Abundance.d: A mindset of plenty and generosity rather than scarcity.
Abundance.e: You share time, resources, and opportunities freely, trusting there's enough.

Academics.d: Valuing formal learning, study, and scholarship.
Academics.e: You pursue degrees, courses, or deep study to master a field.

Accomplishment.d: Completing meaningful things and seeing them through.
Accomplishment.e: You feel most alive when you finish what you set out to do.

Accountability.d: Owning your commitments and answering for your results.
Accountability.e: You follow through on what you say and admit when you fall short.

Accuracy.e: You double-check figures and facts before you sign off.

Achievement.d: Reaching goals and high standards of performance.
Achievement.e: You set stretch targets and push to hit them.

Adventure.d: Seeking new, bold, and stimulating experiences.
Adventure.e: You say yes to the trip, the challenge, the unknown.

Altruism.d: Selfless concern for the well-being of others.
Altruism.e: You give without needing anything in return.

Ambition.d: A strong drive to advance and achieve more.
Ambition.e: You're always reaching for the next level.

Art.d: Creating or appreciating beauty and expression.
Art.e: You make time for music, design, writing, or craft.

Assertiveness.d: Stating your needs and views with confidence.
Assertiveness.e: You speak up for what you need without apology.

Autonomy.d: Freedom to direct your own work and life.
Autonomy.e: You do your best when trusted to work your own way.

Balance.d: Healthy proportion across the areas of your life.
Balance.e: You protect rest and relationships alongside work.

Beauty.d: Valuing what is lovely, elegant, or inspiring.
Beauty.e: You notice and create beauty in your surroundings.

Being Right.d: Having correct answers and sound judgment.
Being Right.e: You research thoroughly so your position holds up.

Belonging.d: Being accepted and connected within a group.
Belonging.e: You invest deeply in your circle and feel at home there.

Boldness.e: You take the risk others hesitate to take.

Bottom-Line.d: Focusing on results, ROI, and what matters most.
Bottom-Line.e: You cut to the outcome and measure what counts.

Calm.d: Inner steadiness and peace under pressure.
Calm.e: You stay level-headed when things get chaotic.

Certainty.e: You want a clear plan before you move.

Challenge.d: Growing through difficulty and testing yourself.
Challenge.e: You seek out problems that stretch you.

Change.d: Embracing new ways and continual reinvention.
Change.e: You get restless with the status quo and push for better.

Clarity.e: You make the complicated simple for others.

Cleanliness.d: Order, tidiness, and purity in your environment.
Cleanliness.e: You keep your space and systems clean and organized.

Cleverness.e: You find the smart shortcut others miss.

Collaboration.d: Working with others toward a shared goal.
Collaboration.e: You bring people together to build something better.

Commitment.d: Devotion and staying power over the long haul.
Commitment.e: You keep going long after the excitement fades.

Communication.d: Sharing ideas clearly and listening well.
Communication.e: You keep people informed and truly hear them.

Community.d: Belonging to and building up a group of people.
Community.e: You show up for your neighbors, church, or team.

Compassion.d: Feeling with others and acting to help.
Compassion.e: You move toward people who are hurting.

Competitiveness.e: You play to win and keep score.

Contentment.d: Peace and gratitude with what you have.
Contentment.e: You can say 'enough' and mean it.

Control.e: You like to steer the plan and reduce surprises.

Cooperation.e: You flex to help the team succeed.

Courage.d: Facing fear and doing hard right things.
Courage.e: You speak or act rightly even when it costs you.

Courtesy.d: Respect and good manners toward others.
Courtesy.e: You treat everyone, at every level, with respect.

Creativity.d: Generating new ideas and original solutions.
Creativity.e: You reimagine problems and invent fresh answers.

Curiosity.d: A hunger to learn, explore, and ask why.
Curiosity.e: You dig into how things work and keep asking questions.

Decisiveness.d: Making clear decisions and moving forward.
Decisiveness.e: You choose and commit rather than stall.

Dependability.e: People know they can count on you, every time.

Details.d: Care for the small, precise parts.
Details.e: You catch the things others overlook.

Determination.d: Persistent resolve to reach your goal.
Determination.e: You don't quit when it gets hard.

Development.d: Growing yourself and others over time.
Development.e: You invest in learning and coaching people forward.

Discipline.e: You do the right thing whether or not you feel like it.

Discovery.d: Finding and uncovering the new or unknown.
Discovery.e: You love the moment of figuring something out.

Diversity.d: Valuing varied people, ideas, and perspectives.
Diversity.e: You seek out voices different from your own.

Education.d: Learning and teaching as a way of life.
Education.e: You keep studying and help others learn.

Efficiency.e: You streamline so nothing is wasted.

Elegance.e: You prefer the clean, simple solution.

Empathy.e: You sense what someone is going through and respond.

Environment.d: Caring for creation and the natural world.
Environment.e: You make choices that protect the planet.

Equality.d: Fair, equal treatment and opportunity for all.
Equality.e: You advocate so everyone gets a fair shot.

Excellence.e: You refuse to settle for 'good enough.'

Expressiveness.d: Openly sharing thoughts, feelings, and creativity.
Expressiveness.e: You say what you feel and let it show.

Fairness.e: You give each person what they truly need.

Faith.d: Trust in God and living from that belief.
Faith.e: You make decisions rooted in your relationship with God.

Family.e: You protect time and energy for the people at home.

Fitness.e: You train and take care of your body.

Flexibility.e: You adjust the plan without losing your footing.

Freedom.e: You value the ability to choose your own path.

Friendships.e: You invest in a few close, lasting friendships.

Fun.e: You make room to laugh and enjoy life.

Future-Oriented.d: Focusing on vision and what's ahead.
Future-Oriented.e: You plan for where things are going, not just today.

Generosity.d: Giving freely of what you have.
Generosity.e: You're quick to give time, money, or help.

Genuineness.e: What people see in you is what they get.

Grace.e: You extend mercy where judgment would be easy.

Gratitude.e: You notice and name the good regularly.

Growth.e: You measure yourself against who you were, not others.

Hard Work.d: Diligence and putting in the effort.
Hard Work.e: You out-work the problem and take pride in it.

Harmony.e: You smooth conflict and keep relationships whole.

Health.e: You make choices that keep you well.

Holiness.d: Being set apart and pure before God.
Holiness.e: You pursue a life that honors God in private and public.

Honesty.e: You tell the truth even when it's uncomfortable.

Honor.d: Living with integrity worthy of respect.
Honor.e: You keep your word and do what's right.

Hospitality.d: Warmly welcoming and caring for others.
Hospitality.e: You open your home and table to people.

Humility.d: A modest, teachable view of yourself.
Humility.e: You give credit away and stay open to correction.

Impact.e: You choose work that changes things for people.

Independence.d: Self-reliance and standing on your own.
Independence.e: You'd rather figure it out yourself.

Individuality.e: You honor what makes you different.

Initiative.e: You start things and don't wait for permission.

Innovation.e: You improve what everyone else accepts as fixed.

Integrity.d: Wholeness — your life matches your values.
Integrity.e: You're the same person in public and in private.

Intelligence.e: You think things through and value being informed.

Intensity.e: You go all-in on what matters to you.

Intimacy.e: You seek relationships of real depth, not just surface.

Intuition.d: Trusting inner sense and gut discernment.
Intuition.e: You often 'just know' the right move.

Journey.d: Valuing the process, not only the destination.
Journey.e: You savor the road and what it forms in you.

Joy.d: Deep gladness that isn't tied to circumstance.
Joy.e: You carry a settled joy through ups and downs.

Justice.d: Standing for what is right and fair.
Justice.e: You act to right wrongs and defend the vulnerable.

Kindness.d: Warmth and care in how you treat people.
Kindness.e: You look for small ways to be kind daily.

Knowledge.e: You love to know how and why things work.

Leadership.d: Guiding and influencing others toward a goal.

Learning.d: Ongoing growth in skill and understanding.
Learning.e: You're always picking up something new.

Legacy.e: You build things meant to outlive you.

Logic.e: You want the argument to make sense before you act.

Love.d: Deep care and commitment to others' good.
Love.e: You put people's real good ahead of your own comfort.

Loyalty.d: Faithful devotion to people and causes.
Loyalty.e: You stand by your people when it's hard.

Mastery.d: Deep skill and expertise in your craft.
Mastery.e: You practice until you're truly excellent.

Meaning.d: Purpose and significance in what you do.
Meaning.e: You need your work to matter, not just pay.

Moderation.e: You enjoy things without overdoing them.

Modesty.e: You downplay your wins and let work speak.

Mystery.d: Comfort with wonder and the unknown.
Mystery.e: You're drawn to what can't be fully explained.

Nature.d: Connection to the outdoors and creation.
Nature.e: You recharge in the natural world.

Nurturing.d: Caring for and growing people or things.
Nurturing.e: You help others flourish and thrive.

Obedience.d: Faithful submission to God or rightful authority.
Obedience.e: You follow through on what you're called to do.

Openness.d: Receptiveness to ideas, people, and change.
Openness.e: You stay curious and hard to offend.

Optimism.e: You see possibility where others see problems.

Patience.d: Calm endurance and willingness to wait.
Patience.e: You give people and processes time to develop.

Patriotism.d: Love and service to your country.
Patriotism.e: You honor and serve your nation.

Peace.d: Inner and relational wholeness and calm.
Peace.e: You seek and create peace around you.

Perfection.e: You refine until there's nothing left to fix.

Perseverance.e: You keep going when others give up.

Planning.e: You map the path before you take it.

Positivity.e: You lift the mood and focus on solutions.

Power.d: Capacity to influence and make things happen.
Power.e: You seek the position to effect real change.

Precision.e: You get it exactly, not approximately, right.

Problem-Solving.e: You're energized by untangling a tough problem.

Professionalism.d: High standards of conduct and quality.
Professionalism.e: You show up prepared, polished, and reliable.

Progress.e: You want to see things getting better over time.

Purpose.d: A clear sense of why you exist.
Purpose.e: You anchor your choices to your deeper why.

Quality.d: Excellence and high standards in output.

Reflection.d: Thoughtful pause to learn and grow.
Reflection.e: You regularly step back to process and pray.

Relationships.d: Connection with people as a priority.
Relationships.e: You measure success by the health of your relationships.

Relaxation.e: You protect time to truly unwind.

Reliability.e: You deliver what you promise, on time.

Religion.d: Faith expressed through practice and community.
Religion.e: Your beliefs shape your daily habits and worship.

Research.e: You dig into the data before deciding.

Resourcefulness.d: Making the most of what you have.
Resourcefulness.e: You find a way with limited means.

Results.e: You judge effort by what it actually produces.

Risk.d: Willingness to bet on uncertain upside.
Risk.e: You take the leap when the reward is worth it.

Security.e: You build a stable foundation for yourself and others.

Self-Control.d: Mastery over your impulses and reactions.
Self-Control.e: You respond thoughtfully instead of reacting.

Selflessness.d: Putting others' needs before your own.
Selflessness.e: You give up your preference for someone else's good.

Sensitivity.e: You read the room and respond with care.

Service.e: You look for ways to serve, not be served.

Simplicity.e: You strip away what isn't needed.

Spirituality.d: Connection to God and the transcendent.
Spirituality.e: You tend to your spiritual life intentionally.

Spontaneity.e: You're happy to change plans on a whim.

Stability.e: You're the steady one others lean on.

Strategic.e: You see the whole board and plan accordingly.

Strength.e: You carry weight others can't and keep standing.

Success.d: Achieving what you set out to accomplish.
Success.e: You define and pursue winning clearly.

Supernatural.d: Openness to God working beyond the natural.
Supernatural.e: You expect and welcome God to move.

Support.e: You're the one cheering people on and helping.

Teamwork.e: You'd rather win as a team than shine alone.

Thought.e: You need time to think things all the way through.

Timeliness.e: You're early, and you meet deadlines.

Tolerance.e: You make room for people unlike you.

Tradition.e: You keep the practices that ground your family or faith.

Trust.e: You extend and keep trust as sacred.

Truth.d: Commitment to what is real and honest.
Truth.e: You pursue and speak the truth, even when hard.

Understanding.e: You seek to truly understand before responding.

Unity.e: You work to bring people together, not divide.

Variety.e: You thrive on mixing it up and trying new things.

Wealth.e: You build resources to provide and give.

Wisdom.e: People come to you for perspective on hard calls.

Adaptability.d: Adjusting well to new and changing conditions.
Adaptability.e: You stay effective when the plan shifts and pivot quickly.

Authenticity.d: Being true to who you really are.
Authenticity.e: You show up as yourself, not a performance.

Connection.e: You prioritize real connection over surface contact.

Forgiveness.e: You let go of grudges and move toward reconciliation.

Influence.e: You use your voice to move people toward good.

Respect.d: Honoring the worth and dignity of others.
Respect.e: You treat every person as valuable, at every level.

Responsibility.d: Owning your duties and their outcomes.
Responsibility.e: You take ownership instead of making excuses.

Stewardship.d: Faithfully managing what you've been entrusted.
Stewardship.e: You care well for your time, money, and resources.
